questions.text: What is the opening declaration of this Surah?
questions.answer: Certainly, the believers have succeeded (Qad aflahal mu'minun).

questions.text: List five characteristics of the successful believers mentioned in the first 10 verses.
questions.answer: Humble in prayer, turning away from ill speech, observant of Zakat, guarding their private parts, and being true to their trusts and covenants.

questions.text: What is the reward for the believers who possess these qualities?
questions.answer: They will inherit Al-Firdaws (the highest Paradise), to dwell therein forever.

questions.text: Describe the stages of the development of the embryo in Verses 12-14.
questions.answer: Nutfah (sperm-drop), Alaqah (clot), Mudghah (lump of flesh), bones, then clothing the bones with flesh, then a new creation.

questions.text: What did Allah create above us in Verse 17?
questions.answer: Seven paths (heavens), and He is never unaware of the creation.

questions.text: What was the command to Nuh (AS) regarding the construction of the Ark?
questions.answer: To construct it 'under Our eyes and Our inspiration'.

questions.text: What happened to the 'generations after Nuh' who rejected their Messengers?
questions.answer: They were seized by the 'Shaykhah' (blast/shout) and made into rubbish/scum.

questions.text: Did the various Messengers bring different religions?
questions.answer: No, Verse 52 states: 'And indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so fear Me'.

questions.text: What is the state of those who give what they give while their hearts are 'fearful' (Verse 60)?
questions.answer: They are fearful because they know they will return to their Lord, so they hasten to do good deeds.

questions.text: How does Allah respond to the disbelievers' claim that there is only the life of this world?
questions.answer: He asks if they thought He created them in play (without purpose) and that they would not be returned to Him.

questions.text: What is the best way to repel evil according to Verse 96?
questions.answer: Repel evil with that which is better.

questions.text: What is the 'Barzakh' mentioned in Verse 100?
questions.answer: A barrier behind the dead until the Day they are resurrected.

questions.text: Will family ties matter on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, when the Trumpet is blown, there will be no relationship between them, nor will they ask about each other (Verse 101).

questions.text: What determines success on the Scale (Mizan)?
questions.answer: Those whose scales are heavy (with good deeds) are the successful; those whose scales are light have lost their souls.

questions.text: How long will the inhabitants of Hell think they stayed on Earth?
questions.answer: A day or part of a day.

questions.text: What will Allah say to the disbelievers when they ask to be taken out of Hell?
questions.answer: 'Remain despised therein and do not speak to Me'.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'Most Merciful' in its final verse?
questions.answer: As the best of those who show mercy.

questions.text: What is the 'sign' regarding the ships (vessels) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They carry you across the seas, just as cattle carry you on land.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers mock the idea of resurrection?
questions.answer: They said, 'When we have died and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?'

questions.text: What is the 'truth' that the disbelievers' hearts are in 'confusion' about (Verse 63)?
questions.answer: The message of the Quran and the deeds they are doing.
